propositions: The enforce_since anchor must be an existing tickets-branch commit that every pre-signing unsigned event strictly precedes, so all historical unsigned events are grandfathered while the anchor and its descendants are enforced.

The grandfathering logic enforces an event iff its introducing commit is the since_ref or a descendant via git merge-base --is-ancestor, failing closed and exiting non-zero when required and enforced-not-verified events exist.

A regression test must drive the boundary through the identity.enforce_since config/env path and assert per-event grandfathered flags in a single mixed store containing pre- and post-boundary unsigned events.

The boundary anchor must be chosen after write-signing is confirmed live across all writers (local, CI reconciler, Gerrit review-bot, enrich drain, completion verifier) and recorded with rationale for the 8d39 flip to consume.
This story does not write enforce_since into rebar.toml; it only decides the anchor and adds the new test file.
AC verification requires rebar verify-identity --all --require-authenticated --since <anchor> to exit 0 with zero enforced non-verified events.
